records.ety: [OE. whennes, whens (with adverbial s, properly a genitive ending; -- see -wards), also whenne, whanene, AS. hwanan, hwanon, hwonan, hwanone; akin to D. when. See When, and cf. Hence, Thence.]

defs: From what place; hence, from what or which source, origin, antecedent, premise, or the like; how; -- used interrogatively.
quotes: Whence hath this man this wisdom?

defs: From what or which place, source, material, cause, etc.; the place, source, etc., from which; -- used relatively.
quotes: Grateful to acknowledge whence his good Descends.
O, how unlike the place from whence they fell?
From whence come wars and fightings among you?
notes: ☞ All the words of this class, whence, where, whither, whereabouts, etc., are occasionally used as pronouns by a harsh construction. [1913 Webster] O, how unlike the place from whence they fell? Milton. [1913 Webster]

☞ From whence, though a pleonasm, is fully authorized by the use of good writers. [1913 Webster] From whence come wars and fightings among you? James iv. 1. [1913 Webster] Of whence, also a pleonasm, has become obsolete. [1913 Webster]
